questions.text: The word 'geometry' comes from the Greek words 'geo' and 'metrein', which mean:
questions.explanation: Geo means Earth and metrein means to measure.

questions.text: According to Euclid, a point is that which has:
questions.explanation: Euclid defined a point as having no dimensions (no length, width, or height).

questions.text: A line is ______ breadthless.
questions.explanation: Euclid defined a line as 'breadthless length'.

questions.text: The ends of a line are:
questions.explanation: The boundaries or ends of a line segment are points.

questions.text: A surface is that which has:
questions.explanation: A surface is 2-dimensional.

questions.text: The edges of a surface are:
questions.explanation: The boundaries of a surface are lines.

questions.text: Euclid divided his famous treatise 'The Elements' into how many chapters?
questions.explanation: The Elements consists of 13 books.

questions.text: Axioms are assumed:
options: Universal truths in all branches of math
questions.explanation: Axioms apply to all mathematics, while postulates are specific to geometry.

questions.text: Postulates are assumed:
questions.explanation: Postulates are the starting assumptions for geometric reasoning.

questions.text: 'Things which are equal to the same thing are equal to one another' is an:
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's first axiom.

questions.text: If equals are added to equals, the wholes are:
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's second axiom.

questions.text: The whole is ______ the part.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's fifth axiom.

questions.text: A straight line may be drawn from any one point to ______.
questions.explanation: Euclid's Postulate 1 states a line can be drawn between any two points.

questions.text: A terminated line can be produced ______.
questions.explanation: Euclid's Postulate 2 allows a line segment to be extended forever.

questions.text: A circle can be drawn with any ______ and any ______.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's Postulate 3.

questions.text: All right angles are ______ to one another.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's Postulate 4.

questions.text: Which postulate deals with parallel lines and the intersection of a transversal?
questions.explanation: Postulate 5 is the famous parallel postulate.

questions.text: A pyramid is a solid figure whose base is:
questions.explanation: The base of a pyramid can be any polygon, and its side faces are triangles.

questions.text: Which of the following needs a proof?
questions.explanation: Theorems are statements proven using axioms, postulates, and logic.

questions.text: Euclid belonged to which country?
questions.explanation: Euclid was a Greek mathematician who lived in Alexandria, Egypt.

questions.text: Boundaries of solids are:
questions.explanation: When you go from 3D to 2D, you get surfaces.

questions.text: How many dimensions does a solid have?
questions.explanation: Solids have length, breadth, and height.

questions.text: How many dimensions does a point have?
questions.explanation: A point has no dimensions.

questions.text: Playfair’s Axiom is an equivalent version of:
questions.explanation: It states that for any line l and point P not on it, there's exactly one line through P parallel to l.

questions.text: Two distinct lines cannot have more than ______ point in common.
questions.explanation: If two lines intersect, they intersect at exactly one point.

questions.text: Euclid’s second axiom (adding equals) is used to solve equations in:
questions.explanation: We often add the same number to both sides of an algebraic equation.

questions.text: Things which are double of the same things are ______ to one another.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's sixth axiom.

questions.text: Things which are halves of the same things are ______ to one another.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's seventh axiom.

questions.text: The number of dimensions in a line is:
questions.explanation: A line has length only.

questions.text: Thales is famous for which discovery?
options: A circle is bisected by its diameter
questions.explanation: Thales was one of the earliest Greek mathematicians to give a formal proof.

questions.text: The side faces of a pyramid are always:
questions.explanation: Regardless of the base, the sides meeting at the apex are triangles.

questions.text: A point is a ______ dimension object.
questions.explanation: Dimensionless means zero dimensions.

questions.text: Given two distinct points, there is a ______ line that passes through them.
questions.explanation: This is an axiom derived from Euclid's first postulate.

questions.text: Geometry originated from the need to:
questions.explanation: Early geometry was used by Egyptians to re-calculate land boundaries after Nile floods.

questions.text: Things which coincide with one another are ______ to one another.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's fourth axiom.

questions.text: The first known proof was given by:
questions.explanation: Thales is credited with the first mathematical proof.

questions.text: Which Greek mathematician was a pupil of Thales?
questions.explanation: Pythagoras studied under Thales and developed many geometric theories.

questions.text: According to Euclid's Postulate 5, if the sum of interior angles is less than two right angles, the lines will eventually:
questions.explanation: They meet on the side where the sum is less than 180 degrees.

questions.text: Euclid’s Postulate 5 is also known as:
questions.explanation: It determines the behavior of parallel lines.

questions.text: Euclid's axioms are used in:
questions.explanation: Axioms are universal mathematical truths.
